element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduction to enemy encounters and puzzle-solving
Core mechanical shift from previous Ys games; requires active input
Four-statue collection drives narrative and level progression
Introduces boss-level encounters and pit escape mechanics
Expands platforming vocabulary with elevated navigation
Complex indoor navigation with hostile NPCs
Combines all prior mechanics with final boss encounter
Escape sequence under time pressure with environmental collapse

typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters (fire dragon, Bishop Garland, MacGuire, Galbalan)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Statuary collection provides episodic tension-relief cycles; each statue quest escalates in difficulty and stakes, with significant relief in town sequences; climactic fortress and island escape compress pacing toward resolution

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low stakes in Redmont and quarry; introduces button-attack system with minimal enemy pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating challenge through Illsburn Ruins and mountain sections; boss encounters (fire dragon) introduce resource management; Chester confrontation raises narrative tension
difficulty_curve.late_game: Valestein Castle navigation requires mastery of all mechanics; hostage plot raises emotional stakes; Galbalan's realm combines platforming precision with multiple boss phases
notable_spikes: Fire dragon encounter in Illsburn Ruins (first major boss spike)
Bishop Garland confrontation in Valestein Castle (late-game combat complexity)
Galbalan boss fight and island escape sequence (sustained high-difficulty finale)

notable_level_patterns.description: Quest structure: player enters location, acquires statue, encounters combat/environmental challenges, returns to town hub for narrative progression. Repeats for three of four statues.
Climbing/descending sections where player must navigate upward or downward while avoiding hazards; exemplified by quarry escape after fire dragon encounter and mountain routes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Quarry pit escape; Mountain hermit approach

notable_level_patterns.description: Indoor castle/fortress navigation requiring precise platforming and combat against multiple enemy types in connected rooms; culminates in boss chamber.
Time-pressured escape through deteriorating space with narrative weight; forces rapid forward movement and decision-making.
